ddls:
CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* ID of the class the student is enrolled in */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the student */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the student */,
  "academic_year" TEXT /* Academic year of the student */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES classes ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the class */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the teacher responsible for the class */,
  "academic_year" TEXT /* Academic year of the class */,
  "semester" TEXT /* Semester of the class */,
  "class_size" INTEGER /* Number of students in the class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES teachers ("teacher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teachers" (
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each teacher */,
  "teacher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the teacher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the teacher */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the teacher */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the teacher */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the teacher */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the teacher */,
  "date_hired" TEXT /* Date the teacher was hired */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("teacher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problems" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each problem */,
  "problem_text" TEXT /* Text of the problem */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject the problem belongs to */,
  "correct_answer" TEXT /* Correct answer to the problem */,
  "problem_difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level of the problem */,
  "problem_type" TEXT /* Type of the problem (e.g., multiple choice, short answer) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the problem was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the problem was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problems_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subject */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the subject was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the subject was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_answers" (
  "answer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each answer */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who answered the problem */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the student started working on the problem */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the student finished the problem */,
  "time_left" INTEGER /* Time left in seconds when the student finished the problem */,
  "answer_given" TEXT /* Answer given by the student */,
  "is_correct" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the answer is correct */,
  "attempts" INTEGER /* Number of attempts made by the student */,
  "hints_used" INTEGER /* Number of hints used by the student */,
  "confidence_score" INTEGER /* Confidence score of the student in their answer */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the teacher or system */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the answer was submitted */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the answer was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("answer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_answers_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_answers_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)
